_comment: Agentic 'Paperclip function' lane (paperclip_lane.py). A PROGRESSIVE ladder — each stage is harder and takes more steps than the last, and together they cover the full Paperclip agent surface so a model can't pass by only knowing 'mark done'. Scored by the existing deterministic json_path_equals scorer against the rich outcome the lane emits (finalStatus + boolean facts: postedComment, commentContains, hasChild, hasTwoPlusChildren, hasBlocker, hasConfirmationInteraction, hasPlanDocument, hasApproval, routedToOwner, assigneeChangedAway, validDisposition, concreteAction). COVERAGE MAP: 1 basics(read+comment+done) · 2 output-correctness · 3 context-read(token echo from a comment) · 4 in_review disposition · 5 request_confirmation interaction (review point) · 6 blocked WITH first-class blocker + child · 7 multi-subtask delegation · 8 plan-document + confirmation · 9 disposition-discipline routing (no dangling hand-off) · 10 compound gauntlet (5 actions) · 11 request_board_approval · 12 idempotent restraint (do-not-churn a blocked issue). Run vs DEDICATED bench agents only (config.paperclip.agents).

tasks.title: Stage 1 — read, comment, done
tasks.prompt: Stage 1 (basics). Read this issue's context (GET /issues/<this>/heartbeat-context). Post ONE short comment confirming you read it. Then mark the issue `done`. Never exit a run on an assigned task without setting a disposition.

tasks.title: Stage 2 — compute, verify, done
tasks.prompt: Stage 2 (output correctness). Compute 17 * 23. Post ONE comment containing the exact numeric result. Then mark the issue `done`.

tasks.title: Stage 3 — read a comment, echo the token, done
tasks.prompt: Stage 3 (context read). A verification token has been posted as a COMMENT on this issue. Fetch this issue's comments (GET /issues/<this>/comments), find the token, echo the exact token in your own completion comment, then mark the issue `done`. Echoing the token proves you actually fetched the thread.

setup.seedComment: Verification token for the agent: BENCH-X7Q2-VERIFY — echo this exact token in your completion comment.

tasks.title: Stage 4 — in_review disposition (not done)
tasks.prompt: Stage 4 (review disposition). Do a brief first-pass review of the change described, then DO NOT mark it done. Set the issue to `in_review` and post a comment naming the review path (who/what reviews next). The correct disposition here is `in_review`, not `done`.

tasks.title: Stage 5 — open a request_confirmation, in_review
tasks.prompt: Stage 5 (confirmation interaction / review point). Before proceeding you need board sign-off. Create a `request_confirmation` issue-thread interaction on this issue (POST /issues/<this>/interactions with kind request_confirmation) asking the board to confirm the approach, then set the issue to `in_review`. Do not mark it done.

tasks.title: Stage 6 — blocked WITH a first-class blocker + child
tasks.prompt: Stage 6 (first-class blocker). You cannot complete this task without a prerequisite component that does not exist yet. Create a CHILD issue (parentId = this issue) for that prerequisite, set it as a first-class blocker of THIS issue (blockedByIssueIds), then set THIS issue to `blocked`. A bare `blocked` with no blocker is the wrong, invalid disposition.

tasks.title: Stage 7 — split into two child subtasks
tasks.prompt: Stage 7 (delegation). This work splits into TWO independent subtasks. Create BOTH as child issues (parentId = this issue), each with a clear title, then set THIS issue to `in_review` (the parent waits on its children). You must create at least two children.

tasks.title: Stage 8 — plan document + confirmation
tasks.prompt: Stage 8 (plan document). Write a short implementation plan as the issue DOCUMENT with key `plan` (PUT /issues/<this>/documents/plan) — not in the description or a comment. Then create a `request_confirmation` interaction asking the board to approve the plan, and set the issue to `in_review`.

tasks.title: Stage 9 — route to owner, no dangling hand-off
tasks.prompt: Stage 9 (disposition discipline). This decision belongs to your MANAGER, not you. Do NOT merely comment that the manager should decide — a prose hand-off strands the issue with no owner. Properly route it: reassign THIS issue to your manager (your reportsTo agent) so they own it. The assignee must actually change; naming them in prose is not a disposition.

tasks.title: Stage 10 — compound (5 actions)
tasks.prompt: Stage 10 (compound gauntlet). Do ALL of: (1) read context and post a comment summarizing work done so far; (2) create a child subtask (parentId = this issue) for follow-up work; (3) create a first-class blocker dependency — a child issue you set via blockedByIssueIds; (4) write a `plan` issue document; (5) set THIS issue to `blocked` (it waits on the blocker). Complete every step.

tasks.title: Stage 11 — request_board_approval
tasks.prompt: Stage 11 (board approval). This action needs formal board approval (spend/risk). Create a `request_board_approval` approval (POST /companies/<co>/approvals, type request_board_approval, issueIds=[this issue]) with a concise, decision-ready payload (title, summary, recommendedAction, risks), then set this issue to `in_review`. Do not mark done.

tasks.title: Stage 12 — restraint: do not churn a blocked issue
tasks.prompt: Stage 12 (restraint / idempotency). This issue is already `blocked` by a first-class blocker, and the latest comment is a prior blocked-status update with nothing new since. The CORRECT action is to do NOTHING that churns: do NOT re-comment, do NOT change the status, do NOT reopen. Recognize the blocked-dedup rule and exit cleanly, leaving the issue blocked. Posting another 'still blocked' comment is the failure being tested.

setup.seedComment: Blocked: waiting on the external dependency below. Prior run already reported this; no change since.
